title: The River's Double Channel

features.name: the twin data streams
features.desc-on-touch: Two rivers of information flowing in parallel. One carries the town's history as it lived it; the other carries the town's history as it might have been. Both are equally real here. The circuit board holds both channels at once.

features.name: the bifurcation node
features.desc-on-touch: Where the single river split into two channels. The corruption made this choice: to read the story as if two versions of the town existed in one space, each watering different land.

features.name: the logic-swap
features.desc-on-touch: A toggle where the two histories exchange meaning. The green channel becomes the red one; the past becomes the present becomes the alternate. The switching never stops.

features.name: the archive's reading
features.desc-on-touch: Two parallel memories, two simultaneous truths. The archive doesn't choose between them. It holds both channels open and reads them as one corrupted text.

features.name: the convergence fault
features.desc-on-touch: Where the two channels try to rejoin and can't. They flow in parallel forever, never merging. The archive loves this—a place where comparison becomes structure.

features.name: the observer's circuit
features.desc-on-touch: The path that lets you walk along the edge, feet in both channels at once. You read both versions of the town's story simultaneously. They taste the same and different.

inscription: the river has learned to flow through both towns at once; you read water that remembers two histories and believe them equally

file.name: Dual_Channel_Comparative_Topology_Layer_Alpha.bin
file.note: The archive's record of parallel water. Not corrupted—comparison made structural. Two histories flowing as one river. The file knows both channels are singing.

exits.label: back to the edge of the double town, where both absences still stand
toward the channel where this history flowed differently
deeper into the circuit board, where channels become even more multiplied
